title: Operation Mpemba effect: Breakdown of resource-Markovianity of free dynamics
authors: Tian-Ren Jin, Yu-Ran Zhang, Heng Fan
abstract: The Mpemba effect refers to faster relaxation of states that are initially farther from equilibrium, yet its characterization is often tied to a chosen distance or resource measure. We introduce resource-Markovianity, an extended concept of quantum Markovianity to quantum resource theories, and formulate the resource Mpemba effect operationally as the breaking of resource-Markovianity by a relaxation operation. This yields a measure-independent operational characterization of resource Mpemba effects in general resource theories, together with quantitative characterizations based on resource-non-Markovianity measures. We illustrate the framework with the Mpemba effect for distinguishability of states, due to its relation to quantum Markovianity, and with the thermomajorization Mpemba effect from an operational perspective. These results reveal a deep interplay between quantum resources, non-Markovianity, and the Mpemba effect.
summary: This work generalizes the Mpemba effect concept by defining 'resource-Markovianity' for quantum resource theories. It shows that the operational detection of this effect occurs when a relaxation process breaks the expected monotonic decrease of a resource measure with time. This provides a unified, measure-independent diagnostic tool applicable to both quantum distinguishability and classical thermodynamic ordering.
main_problem: To provide a measure-independent operational characterization of the resource Mpemba effect by linking it to the breakdown of 'resource-Markovianity' in free dynamics.
main_result: The operational formulation, based on breaking resource-Markovianity, provides a general and measure-independent way to characterize the Mpemba effect across various quantum resource theories.
method: The authors introduce 'resource-Markovianity' as a concept and define the Mpemba effect operationally as the failure of this property during relaxation dynamics.
model_or_system: The framework is applied to general quantum resource theories, modeling dynamics using Lindblad equations for distinguishability (quantum case) and master equations for probability distributions (thermomajorization/classical case).
key_observables: Resource measure $M(ho)$, trace distance $D(ho_1, ho_2)$, and the intermediate matrix norm $L_1(\lambda, t)$.
important_parameters: Time ($t$), resource parameters ($\lambda$), and initial state differences.
main_assumptions: The analysis assumes the existence of inverses for both initialization dynamics ($E_\lambda$) and relaxation operations ($N_t$).
figures_summary: Figures schematically compare three perspectives (state-based, Pontus-Mpemba, operation Mpemba) showing how resource measures change; specific figures show crossovers in trace distance or $L_1$ norms.
paper_structure: The paper develops the concept of resource-Markovianity, establishes the operational definition of the Mpemba effect as its breakdown, and then applies this framework to concrete examples like quantum distinguishability and classical thermomajorization.
why_interesting: It provides a powerful, general mathematical tool (resource-Markovianity) to detect anomalous relaxation phenomena (Mpemba effect) in open quantum systems, linking it directly to non-Markovian dynamics.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Schematic comparison of the state-based, Pontus, and operation perspectives on resource Mpemba effect. The vertical axis denotes the resource measure M(ρ), red circles denote initial resourceful states, green arrows denote free relaxations, and blue circles denote the corresponding output states. (a) State-based Mpemba effect. Different initial states are compared under the same relaxation dynamics, and the more resourceful initial state ρ0 λ1 relaxes to a less resourceful state than the less resourceful initial states ρ0 λ2 and ρ0 λ3. (b) Pontus-Mpemba effect. A two-step protocol consisting of heating stage followed by relaxation is compared with direct relaxation from the same...

FIG. 2. Mpemba effect for distinguishability. The state ρ1, ρ2 are represented by the Bloch vectors ⃗r(i) = (r(i) 1 , r(i) 2 , r(i) 3 )T with r(i) 3 = 0 for i = 1, 2. The trace distance is D(ρ1, ρ2) = 1

FIG. 3. Thermomajorization Mpemba effect from the perspective of operation Mpemba effect. The states ⃗p(λ) are initialized from ⃗p = (1, 0, 0)T by initialization dynamics d⃗p/dλ = ˆA⃗p with the symmetric matrix ˆA = [anm] parametrized by (a01, a12, a02) ≈ (0.010, 0.086, 0.004), ann = P

Figure 3 demonstrates the thermomajorization Mpemba ef- fect with the detector D(⃗p,⃗π) = maxn[pn/πn] of thermoma- jorization [7]. In Fig. 3(a), the detector Dλ,t = D( ˆRt ˆSλ⃗p,⃗π) exhibits the Mpemba effect between different initialization parameters λ, after t > 17. In Fig. 3(b), the increase of L1(λ, t) = ∥ˆRt ˆSλ ˆR−1 t ∥1, which witnesses the athermality of the intermediate matrix, shows that the operation Mpemba effect occur at t ≈5, indicating that the operational signa- tures appear earlier than the state signatures.
